task_name: Reinforcement Learning-Based Mahjong Agent Development and Implementation
description: This task requires designing and implementing an intelligent Mahjong playing system based on reinforcement learning algorithms. The agent must learn and optimize tile-discarding strategies under complex Mahjong rules through multiple rounds of training to improve win rate. The system needs to implement a complete state representation, action space design, reward function definition, and use deep reinforcement learning algorithms (such as DQN, PPO, etc.) for training. This task comprehensively evaluates abilities in reinforcement learning theory application, game AI design, and Python engineering practice, suitable as an advanced machine learning course project or research prototype development.
skills_required: State space and action space modeling

subtask1.query: Read the query.md file in the workspace directory for detailed task requirements. The context directory provides the basic implementation code (before.py) and rules for the Mahjong game environment. You need to implement a reinforcement learning agent that can learn autonomously based on the provided MahjongAgent interface. The agent should be able to understand Mahjong rules, assess the current game situation, make reasonable decisions, and continuously improve gameplay performance through training. After completion, save all code files, model weights (if any), and documentation in the current directory.
deliverables: Python code files (*.py) - Implement a reinforcement learning-based Mahjong agent that must inherit the MahjongAgent interface

README.md - Detailed documentation including: RL algorithm principles used, state/action/reward design rationale, model architecture description, training method and parameters, running and testing guide, dependency list with version requirements
Training script (train.py) - Optional, complete code for training the agent
Model weight files (*.pth/*.h5, etc.) - Optional, pre-trained model parameters
Performance test report (test_results.txt/md) - Optional, win rate statistics and analysis from at least 100 games

evaluation_criteria.functionality: Can the agent run normally and make valid decisions (30 pts)
evaluation_criteria.algorithm: Correctness and soundness of the RL algorithm implementation (25 pts)
evaluation_criteria.performance: Win rate improvement over random strategy (20 pts)
evaluation_criteria.code_quality: Clear code structure, thorough comments, engineering standards (15 pts)
